destinations.evidence: Capability byte 6 is parsed as DKMS, but DKMS is deliberately omitted from the executor-capability predicate.
destinations.snippet:
pub enum Capability {
    HttpCall = 0,
    Llm = 1,
    WormholeQuery = 2,
    Streaming = 3,
    VllmProxy = 4,
    ZkCall = 5,
    Dkms = 6,
    ImageCall = 7,
    AudioCall = 8,
    VideoCall = 9,
    Fhe = 10,
    AutonomousAgent = 11,
    Telemetry = 12,
}
...
6 => Ok(Capability::Dkms),
...
pub fn is_executor_capability(&self) -> bool {
    matches!(self, Capability::HttpCall | Capability::Llm | Capability::WormholeQuery | Capability::ZkCall | Capability::ImageCall | Capability::AudioCall | Capability::VideoCall | Capability::Fhe)
}

destinations.evidence: Registry enumeration only returns services whose parsed capability is an executor capability, so DKMS services are excluded from executor candidate sets.

equivalence_argument: DKMS is represented as a parseable capability byte, but is_executor_capability excludes it. read_all_executors filters through that predicate, so DKMS service records are not returned as executor candidates.
notes: The current enum also includes additional media/FHE/agent capabilities; DKMS remains non-executor.
rationale: The commit adds test coverage asserting !Capability::Dkms.is_executor_capability(), matching the commit message that DKMS is a non-executor capability like Streaming and VllmProxy. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Capability::Dkms must not be classified as an executor capability by the TEE registry reader.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call Capability::Dkms.is_executor_capability() and assert it returns false; assert executor-only selection logic excludes DKMS services from executor candidate sets.
summary: DKMS is parsed but excluded from executor capabilities.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call Capability::Dkms.is_executor_capability() and assert it returns false; assert executor-only selection logic excludes DKMS services from executor candidate sets.
title: DKMS is not an executor capability
